text: Planning a trip is always exciting, but the real adventure begins when you arrive. Experiencing new cultures can be eye-opening, whether it’s observing local customs or exploring unfamiliar streets. Trying local food is one of the best parts—dishes you’ve never tasted often become unforgettable. However, language barriers can make simple tasks, like ordering a meal or asking for directions, a bit challenging. Despite this, these moments add to the experience and often lead to humorous stories. In the end, the memories you collect—the sights, smells, and interactions—last far longer than any souvenir, making every journey truly worthwhile.
